tag: Pro plan pricing and next steps for 30-seat upgrade
summary: The customer confirmed that analytics are driving their interest in upgrading to the Pro plan for 30 seats. The rep offered a discounted annual rate of $32 per seat, contingent on procurement and legal approvals. The rep will send the proposal and data processing agreement (DPA) and involve their security team for review. Procurement approval and legal review on the customer side are required before proceeding. Both parties agreed to regroup early next week, aiming to finalize everything by the end of July.
recommended_next_steps: Customer to identify procurement contact and initiate approval process.
Rep to send proposal and DPA, and involve security team for review.
Schedule follow-up meeting for early next week.

decisions.decision: Proceed with Pro plan at $32 per seat annually, pending procurement and legal approvals.
decisions.evidence: 3: [00:15] Rep: Got it. For 30 seats I'd put you on Pro. List is $40 a seat, but I can do $32 if we sign an annual term.  |  5: [00:31] Rep: Understood. Let's plan on Pro at the annual rate, pending those approvals. I'll send the proposal and the DPA today and loop in our security team for the review.

action_items.task: Send proposal and DPA to customer and loop in security team for review.
action_items.evidence: 5: [00:31] Rep: Understood. Let's plan on Pro at the annual rate, pending those approvals. I'll send the proposal and the DPA today and loop in our security team for the review.

action_items.task: Customer to identify who will handle procurement approval on their side.
action_items.evidence: 8: [00:47] Dana: I'll find out who. Let's regroup early next week.

action_items.task: Set up follow-up meeting for early next week.
action_items.evidence: 8: [00:47] Dana: I'll find out who. Let's regroup early next week.  |  9: [00:50] Rep: Perfect, I'll set up the follow-up and aim to have everything wrapped by end of the month.

blockers.blocker: Procurement approval required on customer side for deals over $25k/year.
